plan.caption:
Three separate cards. One continuous illustration.

Keiko Fukuyama painted a single sweeping scene across the Southern Islands set, and when you place Mew, Pidgeot, and Onix side by side, the sky, clouds, and landscape lock together into one unbroken panorama. Most collectors own one of these. Very few have all three aligned.

The value breakdown, verified at pokeev.com:
. Mew (Southern Islands) — $456
. Pidgeot (Southern Islands) — $41
. Onix (Southern Islands) — $48
. Full panorama — $545 combined

Mew carries almost the entire value here, which tracks. But the triptych as a complete display piece is something different from three loose singles. The art only makes sense whole.

Do you collect connecting-art sets for the panorama, or do you chase the anchor card and move on? Tell us below.

Full card data and expected value for every Southern Islands card at pokeev.com. Link in bio.
